title: approvals — PHP attribute contracts
description: Three attributes. `#[AsApprovableAction]` marks an invokable action class as gated by the approval engine — the `approve` middleware reads this attribute at request time to decide whether to defer execution or pass through. `#[AsApprovalListener]` marks a service as a consumer of approval-lifecycle events for the discovery loader. `#[RegistersApprovableAction]` is a boot-time discovery marker on service providers so a provider can bulk-declare approvable actions in a small footprint (rare — most modules put the attribute on the action class itself). Rejected alternative: `#[RequiresApproval(template:, when:)]` static attribute carrying rule config (design.md D-A4 — templates as code force a deploy to change approver rules; runtime data is the whole point).

attributes.name: AsApprovableAction
attributes.description: Applied to an invokable action class (`__invoke()` handler) whose execution is gated by the approval engine. The `approve` middleware reads the attribute via reflection, looks up matching approval_templates for `(tenant, action_key)`, and either passes through (no matching template) or defers execution (template matches → creates approval_instance → 202 Accepted). At execute-time the same action is dispatched by ApprovalInstanceExecutor with the `scoped('approvals.executing')` flag set, and the middleware passes through to prevent infinite loops.

params.name: actionKey
params.description: Stable dot-separated identifier that names the action. Format: `<namespace>.<domain>.<verb>`. Examples: `access.grants.create`, `billing.refunds.issue`, `identity.impersonation.extend`, `compliance.data.export`, `sports.rosters.delete`. MUST match `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*(\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*)+$`. The registry (`approvable_actions.action_key`) is populated at boot from discovery of every class carrying this attribute.

params.name: label
params.description: Admin-facing display name shown in the tenant template-authoring UI. Null falls back to a humanised `actionKey` (`billing.refunds.issue` → 'Billing / Refunds / Issue'). Prefer explicit labels for shipping-critical actions.

params.name: description
params.description: Long-form operator-facing description of what this action does + why approvals matter. Rendered on the template-authoring UI + on approver-inbox rows. Null falls back to the class-level PHPDoc `@description` block.

params.name: contextSchemaJson
params.description: JSON Schema (or a path to one) describing the shape returned by the action's `approvalContext()` method. Used by (a) the template-authoring UI to power the rule builder, (b) the ExpressionLanguageAdapter to validate `when_expression` compilation against known keys, (c) audit reports to render context values consistently. Missing → the engine treats the context as untyped `Record<string, mixed>` and skips schema-validation.

attributes.name: AsApprovalListener
attributes.description: Marks a service class as an approval-lifecycle listener. The discovery loader scans classes carrying this attribute at boot + wires them to the `Illuminate\Events\Dispatcher` for the declared events. Prefer this to Laravel's static `$listen` array so listener registration stays co-located with the listener class itself.

params.name: events
params.description: FQCN list of `Stackra\Approval\Events\*` classes this listener reacts to. Ordering within the array is not significant. Example: `[ApprovalGranted::class, ApprovalRejected::class]`.

params.name: queue
params.description: Optional queue name for async listener dispatch. Null → runs synchronously in the caller's process (appropriate for critical bridges like `AccessRequestGrantMaterializer`).

params.name: priority
params.description: Higher priority listeners run first. Bridging listeners (`AccessRequestGrantMaterializer`, `PropagateGrantMaterializationOutcome`) declare priority 100 so they run before analytics + notifications sinks.

attributes.name: RegistersApprovableAction
attributes.description: Applied to a service provider class to bulk-declare approvable actions at boot. Used ONLY when a module has a dozen+ approvable actions whose registration would clutter each action class with a per-class attribute (e.g. finance module registering 15 different money-movement actions). The typical case is one `#[AsApprovableAction]` per action class — this attribute is the exception, not the norm.

params.name: actionKey
params.description: Same format as `AsApprovableAction::actionKey`. The registered entry appears in `approvable_actions.action_key`.

params.name: namespace
params.description: Owning module namespace slug (e.g. `access`, `finance`, `identity`). Powers filter-by-namespace in the template-authoring UI + audit reports.

params.name: actionClassFqcn
params.description: Fully-qualified class name of the invokable action. MUST implement `Stackra\Approval\Contracts\ApprovableActionInterface` (declares `approvalContext()` + `approvalLabel()`).

attributes.example:
#[RegistersApprovableAction(actionKey: 'finance.refunds.issue', namespace: 'finance', actionClassFqcn: IssueRefundAction::class, label: 'Issue refund')]
#[RegistersApprovableAction(actionKey: 'finance.credit_memos.issue', namespace: 'finance', actionClassFqcn: IssueCreditMemoAction::class, label: 'Issue credit memo')]
#[RegistersApprovableAction(actionKey: 'finance.chargebacks.contest', namespace: 'finance', actionClassFqcn: ContestChargebackAction::class, label: 'Contest chargeback')]
final class FinanceServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider { /* ... */ }
